A skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.
### Available skills
- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /root/CodexWeb/.codex_users/user_1/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)
- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /root/CodexWeb/.codex_users/user_1/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)
### How to use skills
- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.
- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.
- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.
- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):
  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.
  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.
  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.
  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.
  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.
- Coordination and sequencing:
  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.
  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.
- Context hygiene:
  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.
  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.
  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.
- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.

payload.message: Encontré dos causas en E2E: los tests seguían registrando por UI (frágil con sesión previa) y asumían que el narrador inicial siempre era el host/humano, lo cual no es cierto con la rotación actual. Voy a rehacer ambos tests para autenticar por API y ramificar según quién sea narrador en la primera ronda.

payload.name: exec_command
payload.arguments:
{"cmd":"set -euo pipefail
TS=$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)
BACKUP_DIR="/opt/dixit/.backup_${TS}"
mkdir -p "$BACKUP_DIR"
rsync -a --exclude='.backup_*' --exclude='node_modules' --exclude='client/node_modules' --exclude='client/dist' /opt/dixit/ "$BACKUP_DIR/"
rsync -a --delete \
  --exclude='dixit.db' \
  --exclude='dixit.db-shm' \
  --exclude='dixit.db-wal' \
  --exclude='rooms.json' \
  --exclude='node_modules' \
  --exclude='client/node_modules' \
  --exclude='client/dist' \
  --exclude='test-results' \
  /root/CodexWeb/DIXIT/ /opt/dixit/
cd /opt/dixit && npm ci
cd /opt/dixit/client && npm ci && npm run build
systemctl restart dixit.service
sleep 2
systemctl is-active dixit.service
curl -fsS -o /tmp/dixit_home.html -w "%{http_code}\n" http://127.0.0.1:3200/
curl -fsS -o /tmp/dixit_live.html -w "%{http_code}\n" https://dixit.gamemodai.pro/
echo "BACKUP=$BACKUP_DIR"","yield_time_ms":120000,"max_output_tokens":12000}

payload.user_instructions:
## Skills
A skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.
### Available skills
- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /root/CodexWeb/.codex_users/user_1/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)
- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /root/CodexWeb/.codex_users/user_1/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)
### How to use skills
- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.
- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.
- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.
- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):
  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.
  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.
  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.
  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.
  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.
- Coordination and sequencing:
  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.
  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.
- Context hygiene:
  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.
  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.
  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.
- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.

payload.message:
Hecho. Dejé la app corregida de raíz en bots/audio/estado y **desplegada** en `dixit.gamemodai.pro` con servicio activo.

**Causas raíz (lo que estaba mal)**
- `Solo con bots` reutilizaba el flujo `join` de multijugador y luego `start_with_bots`, por lo que podía enganchar salas previas/estado residual y mezclar modos.
- El estado WS en cliente no se saneaba y podía arrastrar datos inconsistentes (jugadores duplicados/fantasma) al reconectar.
- El audio tenía un bug crítico: el botón “Toca para activar audio” hacía `toggle` (apagaba) en vez de desbloquear reproducción; además dependía de `src` remoto frágil.
- El tic-tac se rearmaba con demasiada frecuencia y podía sonar errático por re-suscripciones de intervalos.

**Qué arreglé**
- Flujo backend nuevo y atómico `start_solo` para crear partida aislada con bots sin pasar por lobby online.
- Refactor de inicio de partida (`applyLobbyGameConfig`, `ensureMinPlayersWithBots`, `initializeGameState`) para evitar inconsistencias entre `start`, `start_with_bots` y `start_solo`.
- Frontend: separación real de intención `solo/online`, eliminación del patrón `soloPending`, limpieza de estado al cambiar sala/fase, reenganche controlado tras reconexión.
- Saneamiento de payload WS en cliente (dedupe de jugadores, guards de `pending/you/board`).
- Audio robusto:
  - estado real (`playing/blocked/error/disabled`) en UI (sin “Música ON” falso),
  - desbloqueo por gesto real (`requestAudioUnlock`),
  - `src` local estable: `/audio/ambient-loop.wav`,
  - tic-tac por segundos restantes (<=10s) sin solapes.
- E2E actualizado a login real y flujo narrador dinámico.

**Archivos principales tocados**
- Backend:
  - [server.js](/root/CodexWeb/DIXIT/server.js:1447)
  - [server.js](/root/CodexWeb/DIXIT/server.js:2098)
- Frontend:
  - [App.jsx](/root/CodexWeb/DIXIT/client/src/App.jsx:42)
  - [App.jsx](/root/CodexWeb/DIXIT/client/src/App.jsx:77)
  - [App.jsx](/root/CodexWeb/DIXIT/client/src/App.jsx:1284)
  - [App.jsx](/root/CodexWeb/DIXIT/client/src/App.jsx:1360)
  - [App.jsx](/root/CodexWeb/DIXIT/client/src/App.jsx:1479)
  - [App.jsx](/root/CodexWeb/DIXIT/client/src/App.jsx:1605)
  - [ambient-loop.wav](/root/CodexWeb/DIXIT/client/public/audio/ambient-loop.wav)
- Tests:
  - [server.integration.test.js](/root/CodexWeb/DIXIT/tests/server.integration.test.js:318)
  - [solo.spec.js](/root/CodexWeb/DIXIT/tests/solo.spec.js:33)
  - [multi.spec.js](/root/CodexWeb/DIXIT/tests/multi.spec.js:34)

**Verificación ejecutada**
- `npm test` (backend integración): **12/12 OK**.
- `npm run test:e2e` (Playwright): **2/2 OK**.
- `npm run build` (frontend): **OK**.

**Producción**
- Sincronizado a `/opt/dixit`, build reconstruida y `dixit.service` reiniciado.
- Estado actual: `active (running)`.
- Checks:
  - `http://127.0.0.1:3200/` -> `200`
  - `https://dixit.gamemodai.pro/` -> `200`
  - `https://dixit.gamemodai.pro/audio/ambient-loop.wav` -> `200`
- Backup previo de despliegue: `/opt/dixit/.backup_20260310_181459`.

Incidencia resuelta durante despliegue: el disco estaba al 100% y SQLite fallaba (`SQLITE_IOERR_SHMSIZE`); liberé espacio seguro y el servicio quedó estable.

content.text:
Hecho. Dejé la app corregida de raíz en bots/audio/estado y **desplegada** en `dixit.gamemodai.pro` con servicio activo.

**Causas raíz (lo que estaba mal)**
- `Solo con bots` reutilizaba el flujo `join` de multijugador y luego `start_with_bots`, por lo que podía enganchar salas previas/estado residual y mezclar modos.
- El estado WS en cliente no se saneaba y podía arrastrar datos inconsistentes (jugadores duplicados/fantasma) al reconectar.
- El audio tenía un bug crítico: el botón “Toca para activar audio” hacía `toggle` (apagaba) en vez de desbloquear reproducción; además dependía de `src` remoto frágil.
- El tic-tac se rearmaba con demasiada frecuencia y podía sonar errático por re-suscripciones de intervalos.

**Qué arreglé**
- Flujo backend nuevo y atómico `start_solo` para crear partida aislada con bots sin pasar por lobby online.
- Refactor de inicio de partida (`applyLobbyGameConfig`, `ensureMinPlayersWithBots`, `initializeGameState`) para evitar inconsistencias entre `start`, `start_with_bots` y `start_solo`.
- Frontend: separación real de intención `solo/online`, eliminación del patrón `soloPending`, limpieza de estado al cambiar sala/fase, reenganche controlado tras reconexión.
- Saneamiento de payload WS en cliente (dedupe de jugadores, guards de `pending/you/board`).
- Audio robusto:

  - desbloqueo por gesto real (`requestAudioUnlock`),
  - `src` local estable: `/audio/ambient-loop.wav`,

- `npm run build` (frontend): **OK**.

**Producción**

- Checks:
  - `http://127.0.0.1:3200/` -> `200`
  - `https://dixit.gamemodai.pro/` -> `200`

- Refactor de inicio de partida (`applyLobbyGameConfig`, `ensureMinPlayersWithBots`, `initializeGameState`) para evitar inconsistencias entre `start`, `start_with_bots` y `start_solo`.
- Frontend: separación real de intención `solo/online`, eliminación del patrón `soloPending`, limpieza de estado al cambiar sala/fase, reenganche controlado tras reconexión.
- Saneamiento de payload WS en cliente (dedupe de jugadores, guards de `pending/you/board`).
- Audio robusto:
  - estado real (`playing/blocked/error/disabled`) en UI (sin “Música ON” falso),
  - desbloqueo por gesto real (`requestAudioUnlock`),
  - `src` local estable: `/audio/ambient-loop.wav`,
  - tic-tac por segundos restantes (<=10s) sin solapes.
- E2E actualizado a login real y flujo narrador dinámico.
